title: ERRO[0004] Failed to start transient timer unit: Unit f6cfd6ea082535d93ea8063060bb5261d73f1cf66fb22d1b6403b9070b7b001c.timer already exists. f6cfd6ea082535d93ea8063060bb5261d73f1cf66fb22d1b6403b9070b7b001c
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
While restarting podman container getting this issue.
[root@ci-5250vm029 ~]# docker restart webui
ERRO[0004] Failed to start transient timer unit: Unit f6cfd6ea082535d93ea8063060bb5261d73f1cf66fb22d1b6403b9070b7b001c.timer already exists.
f6cfd6ea082535d93ea8063060bb5261d73f1cf66fb22d1b6403b9070b7b001c
-->

**Describe the results you received:**
[root@ci-5250vm029 ~]# docker restart webui
ERRO[0004] Failed to start transient timer unit: Unit f6cfd6ea082535d93ea8063060bb5261d73f1cf66fb22d1b6403b9070b7b001c.timer already exists.
f6cfd6ea082535d93ea8063060bb5261d73f1cf66fb22d1b6403b9070b7b001c


**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman container must be restart successfully.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@ci-5250vm029 ~]# podman version

Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.17.7
Built:        Mon Jul 11 14:56:53 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@ci-5250vm029 ~]#
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @atmecs123.

Can you share how the `webui` container has been created?  Since the report indicates that you're running on RHEL.  If you desire a potential backport, please reach out through the Red Hat customer channels (e.g., Bugzilla).

As per this(https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6961531) the issue was seen and was resolved in runc version 1.0.3. Still we are seeing this issue.
webui container was created using below command,
```                
                  podman run -d -it ${options} --read-only --name ${cname} \
                  --network podman ${extra_hosts} \
                  -p ${VIPescaped}:443:443 -p ${VIPescaped}:8443:8443 -p ${VIPescaped}:1443:1443 \
                  -v ${cert_dir}:${cert_dir}:z,ro \
                  -v ${webui_certstore_path}:${webui_certstore_path}:Z,ro \
                  -v ${eca_certstore_path}:${eca_certstore_path}:Z \
                  -v ${smartcard_ca_path}:${smartcard_ca_path}:Z \
                  -v ${usercerts_path}:${usercerts_path}:Z \
                  -v ${i18n_common_path}:${i18n_common_path}:ro \
                  -v ${i18n_gateway_path}:${i18n_gateway_path}:ro \
                  -v ${webui_conf_path}:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:z,ro \
                  -v ${nginx_conf_path}:/etc/nginx/conf.d/infra.conf:z,ro \
                  -v ${nginx_sub_conf_path}:/etc/nginx/conf.d/flex:Z \
                  -v ${upgrade_commit_flag_path}:${upgrade_commit_flag_path}:Z \
                  -v ${software_path}:/mnt/software:z \
                  -v ${webui_tmp_path}:/var/lib/nginx/tmp:Z \
                  --tmpfs /tmp:rw,nodev,nosuid,noexec,size=32m,mode=1777 \
                  -e "ENV_WEBCONSOLE_NAME=${web_console}" \
                  ${image_tag}
               ```
Thank you! In that case, please reach out via the Red Hat customer channels.  There is not much we can do upstream.
